sql_injection.description: SQL Injection: User input is concatenated or interpolated directly into a SQL query without sanitization.
sql_injection.remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements. Never concatenate user input into SQL strings.

cross-site_scripting_xss.description: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS): User input is injected into HTML without sanitization, allowing script execution.
cross-site_scripting_xss.remediation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML, or sanitize input with a library like DOMPurify.

command_injection.description: Command Injection: User input is passed to a system command execution function without validation.
command_injection.remediation: Avoid using exec(), spawn(), or system() with user input. Use allowlists or escape shell arguments.

hardcoded_secret.description: Hardcoded Credentials: API keys, passwords, or tokens found directly in source code.
hardcoded_secret.remediation: Use environment variables or a secure secret management service. Never commit secrets to version control.

insecure_use_of_eval.description: Insecure use of eval() with dynamic or user-controlled input, enabling arbitrary code execution.
insecure_use_of_eval.remediation: Avoid eval(). Use safer alternatives like JSON.parse() or a dedicated parser.

angular_security_bypass.description: Angular DomSanitizer bypass method called with user-controlled input, disabling built-in XSS protection.
angular_security_bypass.remediation: Avoid bypassing Angular's sanitization with untrusted data. Sanitize input before passing to bypass methods.

prototype_pollution.description: Prototype Pollution: User-controlled key used in object property assignment, allowing modification of Object.prototype.
prototype_pollution.remediation: Validate property keys against a whitelist. Reject '__proto__', 'constructor', 'prototype'. Use Map instead of plain objects.

path_traversal.description: Path Traversal: User input used in file system operations without validation, allowing access to arbitrary files.
path_traversal.remediation: Use path.resolve() or path.basename() to normalize paths. Validate the resolved path stays within the intended directory.

open_redirect.description: Open Redirect: User input used in URL redirect without validation, enabling phishing attacks.
open_redirect.remediation: Validate redirect URLs against a whitelist of allowed domains. Use relative paths only.

regular_expression_dos_redos.description: ReDoS: Regular expression with nested quantifiers or overlapping alternation that can cause catastrophic backtracking.
regular_expression_dos_redos.remediation: Rewrite the regex to avoid nested quantifiers. Use atomic groups or the 're2' library for safe regex execution.

insecure_randomness.description: Insecure Randomness: Math.random() used for security-sensitive purposes. It is predictable and not cryptographically secure.
insecure_randomness.remediation: Use crypto.randomBytes() (Node.js) or crypto.getRandomValues() (browser) for security-sensitive random values.
